title: perfumed malaysian fish satay bowl
description: a savory and fluffy bowl dish originating from malaysia, infused with a pescatarian-friendly twist. the kombu-cold-extract and vanilla custard create a unique and refreshing aroma.

ingredients.name: fish satay (white fish, marinated in coconut milk, lime juice, and galangal)
ingredients.notes: use cod or snapper for best results

instructions: start by preparing the fish satay according to the marinating ingredients and cooking instructions.
cook the rice according to package instructions, setting aside to cool.
extract the kombu-cold-extract by combining it with 200ml of hot water and stirring to dissolve, then straining into a clean bowl.
whisk the vanilla beans into the extracted kombu-cold-extract, whisking until well combined.
heat a tablespoon of coconut oil in a small saucepan over medium heat, adding the sugar, fish sauce (if using), lime juice, and galangal, stirring constantly until the sugar dissolves.
add the cooked fish satay to the saucepan, stirring to combine.
to assemble the bowl, arrange a scoop of cooked rice on the bottom, followed by a spoonful of the fish satay mixture, and finishing with a drizzle of the vanilla-kombu-cold-extract.
garnish with chopped coriander leaves, chili flakes (if using), and a sprinkle of coconut flakes.
